skill.name: credits-handler
skill.description: Guides implementation of credit allocation, purchasing, balance display, and deduction checks in an application credit system.
skill.summary: Implement credit purchasing, allocation, balance display, and usage checks with existing app helpers.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many shell execution patterns and two weak cryptography patterns. Manual review found these are false positives from Markdown code spans, fenced TypeScript examples, file path references, and substring matches in prose; no executable shell, cryptography, network, filesystem, or prompt injection behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Markdown Code References
low_findings.description: The external command findings are inline Markdown code spans and fenced TypeScript or TSX examples that document app files, hooks, imports, and helper names. They are not Ruby backtick execution and do not execute commands from this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visibly documentation and example application code inside Markdown. I found no executable script file, shell command, or instruction to run untrusted commands.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Weak Cryptography Substring
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to the YAML description and introductory prose, not to cryptographic APIs or algorithms. No hashing, encryption, or DES-like implementation is present in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe the skill and contain no cryptographic operation. The scanner appears to have matched text substrings rather than code semantics.

content.value_statement: Credit systems can become inconsistent when pricing, purchases, and deductions are implemented separately. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the existing credit helpers and UI patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains where credit types and pricing metadata are configured.
Shows how to build a buy credits UI with the documented hook.
Describes how to display user credit balances in the frontend.
Identifies backend helpers for plan allocation, adding credits, and deducting credits.
Highlights idempotent payment references for credit additions.
Points to balance checks before paid or metered actions run.
limitations: It documents patterns and does not include executable automation.
It assumes the host project already has the referenced credit files.
It does not validate payment provider credentials or webhook signatures.
It does not replace a financial or accounting audit.

use_cases.title: Add a New Credit Type
use_cases.description: Guide a developer through updating schemas, pricing metadata, and plan allocation rules for a new metered feature.

use_cases.title: Build a Purchase Interface
use_cases.description: Help a frontend engineer create a purchase card that calculates prices and sends users to checkout.

use_cases.title: Review Credit Deduction Flow
use_cases.description: Help a product engineer check that balance validation, debit operations, and idempotent credit additions are handled consistently.

prompt_templates.title: Find Credit Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the credits-handler skill, identify the main files I should inspect before changing credit pricing.

prompt_templates.title: Add Credit Type
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the credits-handler skill, guide me through adding a new credit type for audio generation with pricing and plan allocation notes.

prompt_templates.title: Create Purchase UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the credits-handler skill, help me build a buy credits component that shows price, loading state, errors, and checkout behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Deduction Safety
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the credits-handler skill, review my credit deduction flow for balance checks, duplicate additions, and safe transaction references.

output_examples.input: I need to add credits for a new video feature.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists the credit type schema, pricing configuration, optional plan allocation, and balance check points to review.

output_examples.input: I need a page where users can buy more image credits.
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines the purchase hook, package state, checkout provider choice, loading state, error display, and disabled purchase behavior.

output_examples.input: I want to prevent duplicate credit grants from payment webhooks.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a stable payment reference, allocation helper usage, and checks around repeated webhook delivery.

best_practices: Use existing helper functions instead of editing cached user balances directly.
Pass a stable payment or transaction reference when adding credits.
Check balances before running any metered action that deducts credits.
anti_patterns: Do not manually update cached credit balances outside the documented helpers.
Do not add credits from payment events without an idempotency reference.
Do not build purchase UI that ignores loading, error, or disabled states.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. It is Markdown documentation with TypeScript and TSX examples.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it add a payment provider for me?
faq.answer: No. It references provider selection but does not implement provider setup.

faq.question: Does it handle credit balance display?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents the balance hook and a simple display pattern.

faq.question: Does it cover backend allocation?
faq.answer: Yes. It identifies the allocation and recalculation helpers used by routes and webhooks.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found the static alerts to be documentation false positives.
